title: dortmanna coronopifolia var. ceratophylla
intro: Dortmanna coronopifolia var. ceratophylla, also known as the Ceratophylla or Water St. John's Wort, is a variant of the Dortmanna coronopifolia plant species that has adapted to grow in aquatic environments. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Dortmanna coronopifolia var. ceratophylla include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
